name: Cardamom
specimenCopy: Picked before the pods split, dried at low temperature so the seeds keep the oils that define the flavour. Most cardamom loses a measurable amount of those oils in aggressive drying. This one doesn't.
offeringCopy: From the hills named for it. Nowhere else grows this quite the same way.

sensoryProfile.aroma: Sweet camphor, menthol, crushed eucalyptus leaf
sensoryProfile.flavor: Warm, highly aromatic citrus with a clean, spicy-floral finish
sensoryProfile.appearance: Plump, pale-green pods, containing oily black seeds
